content: geographiclib (Karney's algorithm) is the most accurate geodesic library, used as the reference/default by GDAL, pyproj, and geopy internally for exact geodesic distance/bearing, and always succeeds even for antipodal/nearly-antipodal points. pyproj wraps the PROJ C library (broader CRS/projection support, needed for map projections and datum transforms) but for pure point-to-point great-circle distance/bearing/direct-and-inverse geodesic problems, geographiclib is lighter-weight, pure-Python-friendly, and has no external C library/data-file dependency. For an ATC sim only needing lat/lon distance, bearing, and fix-radial-distance math (no map projection/CRS transforms), geographiclib is the better-suited, simpler dependency.
